One of the first things I do after creating a new Node.js project isnтАЩt writing routes or installing Express тАФ itтАЩs configuring ESLint and Prettier.
Many developers consider formatting a personal preference. In reality, consistent formatting is a team productivity tool. When every file follows the same standards, developers spend less time debating style and more time solving business problems.
In this article, IтАЩll explain why every Node.js project should include ESLint and Prettier from day one, the standards I follow, and how they improve code quality.
What are ESLint and Prettier?
Although theyтАЩre often used together, they solve different problems.
ESLint
ESLint is a static analysis tool that finds problems in your JavaScript or TypeScript code.
Examples include:

тАв Unused variables

тАв Missing semicolons

тАв Wrong quote style

тАв Accidental globals

тАв Undefined variables

тАв Potential bugs

тАв Code quality issues
Example:
const name = 'Ajay'console.log(message)ESLint reports:

тАв Missing semicolon

тАв message is not defined
Prettier
Prettier is an opinionated code formatter. Instead of checking your code, it rewrites it into a consistent style.
For example: Before:
const user={name:"Ajay",age:39}After:
const user = {
name: 'Ajay',
age: 39,
};You donтАЩt manually format your code anymore тАФ Prettier does it every time you save.
Why Use Both?
A common misconception is that Prettier replaces ESLint.
It doesnтАЩt.
Think of them like this:
+ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ -+ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ -+
| Tool | Responsibility |
+ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ -+ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ -+
| ESLint | Finds coding mistakes and enforces standards |
| Prettier | Formats your code consistently |
+ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ -+ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ тАФ -+
Together they provide clean, readable, and reliable code.
Coding Standards I Use for Node.js Projects
These are the conventions I follow across my Express and microservice projects.
1. Single Quotes
const name = 'Ajay';Instead of:
const name = "Ajay";Configuration:
"prettier.singleQuote": true2. Semicolons
const port = 3000;Configuration:
"prettier.semi": true3. Two-Space Indentation
Node.js projects commonly use two spaces.
if (user) {
console.log(user.name);
}Configuration:
"prettier.tabWidth": 2,
"prettier.useTabs": false4. Trailing Commas
Trailing commas make Git diffs cleaner.
const user = {
name: 'Ajay',
age: 29,
};Configuration:
"prettier.trailingComma": "es5"5. Maximum Line Length
Long lines reduce readability.
I prefer:
"prettier.printWidth": 1006. Consistent Arrow Functions
(users) => users.length;instead of
users => users.length;Configuration:
"prettier.arrowParens": "always"7. Unix Line Endings
This avoids unnecessary Git changes when developers use different operating systems.
"prettier.endOfLine": "lf"Useful ESLint Rules
Besides formatting, ESLint should help prevent mistakes.
Some useful rules include:
{
"quotes": ["error", "single"],
"semi": ["error", "always"],
"eqeqeq": "error",
"no-unused-vars": "warn",
"no-console": "off",
"prefer-const": "error",
"curly": "error"
}These rules catch many common issues before your code reaches production.

Enable Automatic Formatting
One of the best productivity improvements is formatting on save.
VS Code settings:
{
"editor.formatOnSave": true,
"editor.codeActionsOnSave": {
"source.fixAll.eslint": "always"
}
}Now every time you press Ctrl + S, your editor:

тАв Formats the code

тАв Fixes many ESLint issues automatically

тАв Keeps your project consistent
Benefits for Teams
Using ESLint and Prettier together provides several advantages:

тАв Consistent code style across the entire project

тАв Easier code reviews

тАв Fewer unnecessary Git conflicts

тАв Faster onboarding for new developers

тАв Automatic formatting instead of manual formatting

тАв Early detection of common programming mistakes

тАв Cleaner pull requests
Most importantly, developers stop discussing formatting and focus on solving real engineering problems.
Final Thoughts
Good code isnтАЩt just about making software work тАФ itтАЩs also about making it easy to read, maintain, and improve.
By adopting ESLint and Prettier from the beginning of a project, you establish a consistent coding standard that scales as your team and codebase grow.
Whether youтАЩre building a small Express API or a large microservices platform, investing a few minutes in your development tooling will save hours of manual formatting, reduce code review noise, and improve overall code quality.
Clean code starts with consistent standards, and ESLint plus Prettier is one of the simplest ways to achieve that.
